sourceMovie.title: Identity Thief
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Identity Thief works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Identity Thief clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Identity Thief?
faq.answer: Try Hot Fuzz, Kiss Kiss Bang Bang, The Old Man & the Gun—each shares DNA with Identity Thief while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Identity Thief?
faq.answer: Identity Thief (2013) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Identity Thief worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
